solutions.solution: Let us check all the options one by one. From option (a), 36 + 6 - 3 × 2 = 20 ⟹ 36 + 6 × 3 + 2 = 20 ⟹ 18 + 2 = 20 ⟹ 20 = 20 ⟹ LHS = RHS So, option (a) is correct and there is no need to check other options.
I. From given statement, R > Q ≤ P ⟹ R ≤ P. So, Conclusion I is not true. II. From given statement, S ≤ Q < T ≤ V ⟹ S < V. So, Conclusion II is true.
I. From given statement, P > Q ≥ S ⟹ S ≤ P. So, Conclusion I is true. II. From given statement, V ≥ T > Q > R ⟹ V > R. So, Conclusion II is true.
The given statements are H < I ≤ J, R ≥ I > A. I. From given statement, H < I ≤ R ⟹ H < R. So, Conclusion I is true. II. From given statement, H < I > A. So, no relation can be found between H and A.
I. From given statement, R ≥ I ≤ J. So, no relation can be established between J and R. II. From given statement, A < I ≤ J ⟹ J > A. So, Conclusion II is true.

Given statements are J = K ≥ L ≤ M, K < S, J > T. From given statements, L ≤ K < S ⟹ L < S (true). So, Conclusion I is true. II. From given statements, T < J = K ≥ L ≤ M. So, no relation can be established between M and T.
Given statements are P < R ≥ I = C, L > E ≥ R. From given statements, E ≥ R ≥ I = C ⟹ E ≥ C. So, either E > C or E = C. So, either Conclusion I or II is true.
Given statements are B ≤ C = D > E, F > C. From given statements, B ≤ C < F or B ≤ F (false) and B ≤ C = D > E. C ≥ E (false). So, neither Conclusion I nor II is true.
